(916; 742-7371 
November 16, 1979 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 
W. p. RICH (18S0-196S) 
RICHARD H. FUIOGE {!906- !976} 
RE: 
Request of Ci ty Councilperson 
Southard·- Yuba City 
Council, as to whether or not he has a prohibited 
conflict of interest in voting upon legislation 
currently being studied by the City Planning Commission 
governing criteria and regulations on condominimum 
conversions. 
Gentlemen: 
This office, as Contract Attorneys for the City of Yuba City 
have been specifically requested by Councilperson 
Southard, 
a member of the Yuba City Council, to request an opinion 
from the Fair Political Practices Commission as to whether 
or not Mr. Southard has a prohibited conflict of interest 
in voting upon legislation, urgency or otherwise, that is 
contemplated by the City of Yuba City, covering standards 
and criteria governing condominimum conversions within the 
corporate limits of the City of Yuba City. 
Councilman Southard is a member of the firm of Southard, Marr 
& Tomlinson, who are in the real estate brokerage business 
and also in the real estate investment business in this 
community who have financial interests in existing apartment 
complexes within the City of Yuba City that may be affected 
by this legislation in the event those apartments were the 
subject of requested condominimum conversions. 
Other than 
that, Councilman Southard, to our best knowledge, has no 
different an interest than any other realtor and/or investor 
in this community would have with reference to adoption of 
regulations governing condominimum conversions and the cir-
cumstances and conditions under which the City would permit 
such. 
For those reasons, I have advi 
Councilman Southard, that 
I do not be 
eve that his interest is a 
st 


FAIR POLITICAL 
PRACICES COMMISSION 
November 16,1979 
Page Two 
within the meaning of Conflict of Interest Statutes and he 
could vote upon such legislation and if he felt it prudent 
declare the fact that he has financial interests in ex-
isting apartment complexes within the City, but does not feel 
that that interest is any different than any other realtor 
and/or investor and/or owner of apartment projects and pro-
ceed to vote on such legislation. 
Councilperson Southard, however, feels that the issue is 
of sufficient importance that he would request an opinion 
from the Commission on this issue. 
Your early attention to this would be appreciated in view 
of the fact that we anticipate that this matter will be 
before the Council for consideration of legislation within 
the next couple of weeks. 
yours, 
JOHN SANBROOK 
JS/mlf

sections.question: Whether or not Councilperson Southard has a prohibited conflict of interest in voting upon legislation currently being studied by the City Planning Commission governing criteria and regulations on condominium conversions, given that he is a member of the firm of Southard, Marr & Tomlinson, which is in the real estate brokerage and investment business with financial interests in existing apartment complexes within the City of Yuba City that may be affected by such legislation.
sections.question_synthetic: Does Councilperson Southard face a prohibited conflict of interest in voting on condominium conversion regulations when his real estate firm has financial interests in apartment complexes that could be affected by such legislation?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No CONCLUSION section is present. The document contains the question and background facts but awaits FPPC's response. The requesting attorney indicates he personally believes no conflict exists but defers to FPPC for formal opinion. Some OCR artifacts present in the document text.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether or not Councilperson Southard has a prohibited conflict of interest in voting upon legislation currently being studied by the City Planning Commission governing criteria and regulations on condominium conversions, given that he is a member of the firm of Southard, Marr & Tomlinson, which is in the real estate brokerage and investment business with financial interests in existing apartment complexes within the City of Yuba City that may be affected by such legislation.

Gentlemen: This office, as Contract Attorneys for the City of Yuba City have been specifically requested by Councilperson Southard, a member of the Yuba City Council, to request an opinion from the Fair Political Practices Commission as to whether or not Mr. Southard has a prohibited conflict of interest in voting upon legislation, urgency or otherwise, that is contemplated by the City of Yuba City, covering standards and criteria governing condominimum conversions within the corporate limits of the City of Yuba City. embedding.summary: This is an incoming request from attorneys for the City of Yuba City seeking FPPC guidance on whether Councilperson Southard has a prohibited conflict of interest in voting on proposed condominium conversion legislation, given his financial interests in existing apartment complexes through his real estate firm.
